title: Test Strategy, Contracts, Properties, and Mutation · Repository Factory
summary: Design a layered test suite around risks rather than file counts. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a risk-to-test coverage matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign unit, integration, contract, browser, system, and acceptance tests to distinct risks.
Test public behavior, invariants, boundaries, and failure recovery.
Use generated cases for algebraic properties, parser boundaries, and state invariants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-flaky-tests-l17u8o: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Flaky Tests into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-flaky-tests-l17u8o:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Flaky Tests structural check is only one gate. a risk-to-test coverage matrix must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
